```text
18	export const entryMatches = (
19	  hit: WordHit,
20	  filter: WordFilter,

22	  if (hit.region !== filter.region) return false;
23

25	    return false;

125	
126	const loadCurriculum = async (
127	  region: Region,
128	): Promise<CurriculumEntry[]> => {
129	  if (curriculumCache[region]) return curriculumCache[region];
130	  const chunks = await Promise.all(
131	    Object.values(loadersForRegion(region)).map((load) => load()),
132	  );
133	  const flat = chunks.flatMap((c) => c.default);
134	  curriculumCache[region] = flat;
135	  return flat;
136	};
137	
138	const draftToHit = (d: DraftEntry): WordHit => ({
139	  word: d.word,
140	  region: d.region,
141	  level: d.level,
142	  syllableCount: d.syllableCount,
143	  syllables: d.syllables,
144	  variants: d.variants,
145	  ipa: d.ipa || undefined,
146	  graphemes: d.graphemes,
147	  provenance: 'draft' satisfies Provenance,
148	  draftId: d.id,
149	});
150	
151	const joinHits = (
152	  curriculum: CurriculumEntry[],
153	  core: Map<string, WordCore>,
154	  region: Region,
155	): WordHit[] =>
156	  curriculum.flatMap((entry) => {
157	    const c = core.get(entry.word);
158	    if (!c) return [];
159	    return [
160	      {
161	        word: entry.word,
162	        region,
163	        level: entry.level,
164	        syllableCount: c.syllableCount,
165	        syllables: c.syllables,
166	        variants: c.variants,
167	        ipa: entry.ipa || undefined,
168	        graphemes: entry.graphemes,
169	        provenance: 'shipped',
170	      } as WordHit,
171	    ];
172	  });
173	
174	const sortByWord = (hits: WordHit[]): WordHit[] =>
175	  hits.toSorted((a, b) => a.word.localeCompare(b.word));
176	
177	export const loadShippedIndex = async (
178	  region: Region,
179	): Promise<Set<string>> => {
180	  const curriculum = await loadCurriculum(region);
181	  return new Set(curriculum.map((entry) => entry.word));
182	};
183	
184	export const filterWords = async (
185	  filter: WordFilter,
186	): Promise<FilterResult> => {
187	  const core = await loadCore();
188	  const curriculum = await loadCurriculum(filter.region);
189	  const shipped = joinHits(curriculum, core, filter.region).filter(
190	    (h) => entryMatches(h, filter),
191	  );
192	
193	  // Lazy import to avoid Dexie singleton conflicts in environments
194	  // that also load RxDB (which bundles a different Dexie version).
195	  const { draftStore } = await import('./authoring/draftStore');
196	  const allDrafts = await draftStore.listDrafts({
197	    region: filter.region,
198	  });
199	  const drafts = allDrafts
200	    .map((d) => draftToHit(d))
201	    .filter((h) => entryMatches(h, filter));
202	
203	  if (
204	    shipped.length > 0 ||
205	    drafts.length > 0 ||
206	    filter.region === 'aus' ||
207	    filter.fallbackToAus === false
208	  ) {
209	    return { hits: sortByWord([...shipped, ...drafts]) };
210	  }
211	
212	  const ausCurriculum = await loadCurriculum('aus');
213	  const ausShipped = joinHits(ausCurriculum, core, 'aus').filter((h) =>
214	    entryMatches(h, { ...filter, region: 'aus' }),
215	  );
216	  const ausDrafts = await draftStore.listDrafts({ region: 'aus' });
217	  const ausFilteredDrafts = ausDrafts
218	    .map((d) => draftToHit(d))
219	    .filter((h) => entryMatches(h, { ...filter, region: 'aus' }));
220	  return {
221	    hits: sortByWord([...ausShipped, ...ausFilteredDrafts]),
222	    usedFallback: { from: filter.region, to: 'aus' },
223	  };
224	};
225
```

```text
51	export const matchesWordPrefix = (
52	  hit: WordHit,
53	  prefix: string,
54	): boolean => {
55	  const q = prefix.trim().toLowerCase();
56	  if (q === '') return true;
57	  return hit.word.toLowerCase().startsWith(q);
58	};

60	export const paginate = <T,>(
61	  items: readonly T[],
62	  page: number,
63	  pageSize: number,
64	): T[] => {
65	  if (pageSize <= 0) return [];
66	  const start = Math.max(0, (page - 1) * pageSize);
67	  return items.slice(start, start + pageSize);
68	};
69	
70	export type PageToken = number | 'ellipsis-left' | 'ellipsis-right';
71	
72	export const pageRange = (
73	  current: number,
74	  total: number,
75	): PageToken[] => {
76	  if (total <= 1) return [1];
77	  if (total <= 7) {
78	    return Array.from({ length: total }, (_, i) => i + 1);
79	  }
80	  const windowStart = Math.max(2, current - 1);
81	  const windowEnd = Math.min(total - 1, current + 1);
82	  const tokens: PageToken[] = [1];
83	  if (windowStart > 2) tokens.push('ellipsis-left');
84	  for (let p = windowStart; p <= windowEnd; p += 1) tokens.push(p);
85	  if (windowEnd < total - 1) tokens.push('ellipsis-right');
86	  tokens.push(total);
87	  return tokens;
88	};

90	interface GraphemePair {
91	  g: string;
92	  p: string;
93	  label: string;

98	const pairLabel = (g: string, p: string): string => `${g}[${p}]`;
99	
100	export const collectGraphemePairs = (
101	  hits: readonly WordHit[],
102	): GraphemePair[] => {
103	  const seen = new Map<string, GraphemePair>();
104	  for (const hit of hits) {
105	    if (!hit.graphemes) continue;
106	    for (const gr of hit.graphemes) {
107	      const label = pairLabel(gr.g, gr.p);
108	      if (!seen.has(label))
109	        seen.set(label, { g: gr.g, p: gr.p, label });
110	    }
111	  }
112	  return [...seen.values()].toSorted((a, b) =>
113	    a.label.localeCompare(b.label),
114	  );
115	};
116	
117	export const collectGraphemeStrings = (
118	  hits: readonly WordHit[],
119	): string[] => {
120	  const seen = new Set<string>();
121	  for (const hit of hits) {
122	    if (!hit.graphemes) continue;
123	    for (const gr of hit.graphemes) seen.add(gr.g);
124	  }
125	  return [...seen].toSorted();
126	};

133	    if (!hit.graphemes) continue;
134	    for (const gr of hit.graphemes) seen.add(gr.p);
135	  }
136	  return [...seen].toSorted();
137	};

331	const keyGraphemes = (graphemes: Grapheme[]): KeyedGrapheme[] => {
332	  const counts = new Map<string, number>();
333	  return graphemes.map((gr) => {
334	    const base = `${gr.g}-${gr.p}`;
335	    const n = counts.get(base) ?? 0;
336	    counts.set(base, n + 1);
337	    return { key: `${base}-${n}`, g: gr.g, p: gr.p };
338	  });
339	};

416	const updateLevels = (
417	  current: readonly number[] | undefined,
418	  level: number,
419	): number[] => {
420	  const set = new Set(current);
421	  if (set.has(level)) set.delete(level);
422	  else set.add(level);
423	  return [...set].toSorted((a, b) => a - b);
424	};

501	          onGraphemePairsChange={setGraphemePairs}
502	          graphemesAllowed={filter.graphemesAllowed ?? []}
503	          onGraphemesAllowedChange={(next) =>
504	            setFilter((f) => ({
505	              ...f,
506	              graphemesAllowed: next.length > 0 ? next : undefined,
507	            }))
508	          }
509	          graphemesRequired={filter.graphemesRequired ?? []}
510	          onGraphemesRequiredChange={(next) =>
511	            setFilter((f) => ({
512	              ...f,
513	              graphemesRequired: next.length > 0 ? next : undefined,
514	            }))
515	          }
516	          phonemesAllowed={filter.phonemesAllowed ?? []}
517	          onPhonemesAllowedChange={(next) =>
518	            setFilter((f) => ({
519	              ...f,
520	              phonemesAllowed: next.length > 0 ? next : undefined,
521	            }))
522	          }
523	          phonemesRequired={filter.phonemesRequired ?? []}
524	          onPhonemesRequiredChange={(next) =>
525	            setFilter((f) => ({
526	              ...f,
527	              phonemesRequired: next.length > 0 ? next : undefined,
528	            }))
529	          }
530	        />
531	      </CardContent>
532	    </Card>
533	  );
534	};
535	
536	export const WordLibraryExplorer = () => {
537	  const [filter, setFilter] = useState<WordFilter>({
538	    region: 'aus',
539	    fallbackToAus: true,
540	  });
541	  const [result, setResult] = useState<FilterResult>({ hits: [] });
542	  const [syllableMode, setSyllableMode] = useState<SyllableMode>('any');
543	  const [graphemePairs, setGraphemePairs] = useState<GraphemePair[]>(
544	    [],
545	  );
546	  const [wordPrefix, setWordPrefix] = useState('');
547	  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);
548	  const [pageSize, setPageSize] = useState<PageSize>(DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE);
549	  const chipsDefault = useChipsVisibleDefault();
550	  const [chipsVisible, setChipsVisible] =
551	    useState<boolean>(chipsDefault);
552	  const [chipsUserSet, setChipsUserSet] = useState(false);
553	  const [authoringWord, setAuthoringWord] = useState<string | null>(
554	    null,
555	  );
556	  const [showDrafts, setShowDrafts] = useState(false);
557	  const [draftCount, setDraftCount] = useState(0);
558	  // Adjust during render instead of in an effect to avoid cascading renders
559	  // (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect). Until the user explicitly toggles,
560	  // follow the orientation-driven default from useChipsVisibleDefault.
561	  const [prevChipsDefault, setPrevChipsDefault] =
562	    useState(chipsDefault);
563	  if (chipsDefault !== prevChipsDefault) {
564	    setPrevChipsDefault(chipsDefault);
565	    if (!chipsUserSet) setChipsVisible(chipsDefault);
566	  }
567	
568	  useEffect(() => {
569	    let cancelled = false;
570	    filterWords(filter).then((next) => {
571	      if (!cancelled) setResult(next);
572	    });
573	    return () => {
574	      cancelled = true;
575	    };
576	  }, [filter]);
577	
578	  useEffect(() => {
579	    let ignore = false;
580	    void draftStore.listDrafts({ region: 'aus' }).then((d) => {
581	      if (!ignore) setDraftCount(d.length);
582	    });
583	    return () => {
584	      ignore = true;
585	    };
586	  }, [authoringWord, showDrafts]);
587	
588	  const pairOptions = useMemo(
589	    () => collectGraphemePairs(result.hits),
590	    [result.hits],
591	  );
592	  const graphemeOptions = useMemo(
593	    () => collectGraphemeStrings(result.hits),
594	    [result.hits],
595	  );
596	  const phonemeOptions = useMemo(
597	    () => collectPhonemeStrings(result.hits),
598	    [result.hits],
599	  );
600	
601	  // graphemePairs and wordPrefix are client-only post-filters; the WordFilter
602	  // API does not support (g,p) tuples or word-text filtering yet, so we apply
603	  // them after filterWords resolves.
604	  const postFiltered = useMemo(
605	    () =>
606	      result.hits.filter(
607	        (hit) =>
608	          matchesWordPrefix(hit, wordPrefix) &&
609	          (graphemePairs.length === 0 ||
610	            hitContainsAllPairs(hit, graphemePairs)),
611	      ),
612	    [result.hits, graphemePairs, wordPrefix],
613	  );
614	
615	  const totalBeforeFilters = result.hits.length;
616	  const matched = postFiltered.length;
617	  const totalPages = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(matched / pageSize));
618	
619	  // Reset to page 1 when any pagination input changes. Using React's
620	  // "adjust state during render" pattern instead of an effect to avoid
621	  // cascading re-renders (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect).
622	  const resetKey = `${filter.region}|${(filter.levels ?? []).join(',')}|${(filter.levelRange ?? []).join('-')}|${filter.syllableCountEq ?? ''}|${(filter.syllableCountRange ?? []).join('-')}|${filter.fallbackToAus}|${(filter.graphemesAllowed ?? []).join(',')}|${(filter.graphemesRequired ?? []).join(',')}|${(filter.phonemesAllowed ?? []).join(',')}|${(filter.phonemesRequired ?? []).join(',')}|${graphemePairs.map((p) => p.label).join(',')}|${wordPrefix.trim().toLowerCase()}|${pageSize}`;
623	  const [prevResetKey, setPrevResetKey] = useState(resetKey);
624	  if (resetKey !== prevResetKey) {
625	    setPrevResetKey(resetKey);
626	    setPage(1);
627	  }
628	
629	  // Clamp during render so a stale page past the new bounds shows a valid
630	  // slice instead of an empty grid.
631	  const effectivePage = Math.min(Math.max(1, page), totalPages);
632	  const visible = paginate(postFiltered, effectivePage, pageSize);
633	  const rangeStart =
634	    matched === 0 ? 0 : (effectivePage - 1) * pageSize + 1;
635	  const rangeEnd = Math.min(matched, effectivePage * pageSize);
636	
637	  const setSyllables = (mode: SyllableMode) => {
638	    setSyllableMode(mode);
639	    setFilter((f) => ({
640	      ...f,
641	      syllableCountEq: undefined,
642	      syllableCountRange: undefined,
643	    }));
644	  };
645	
646	  const pills = deriveActiveFilterPills(
647	    filter,
648	    graphemePairs,
649	    wordPrefix,
650	  );
651	
652	  const clearFilterPill = (clearKey: string) => {
653	    if (clearKey === 'prefix') {
654	      setWordPrefix('');
655	      return;
656	    }
657	    if (clearKey.startsWith('pair:')) {
658	      const label = clearKey.slice('pair:'.length);
659	      setGraphemePairs((curr) => curr.filter((p) => p.label !== label));
660	      return;
661	    }
662	    setFilter((f) => ({ ...f, [clearKey]: undefined }));
663	  };
664	
665	  const filtersPanelProps: FiltersPanelProps = {
666	    wordPrefix,
667	    setWordPrefix,
668	    filter,
669	    setFilter,
670	    syllableMode,
671	    setSyllableMode: setSyllables,
672	    graphemePairs,
673	    setGraphemePairs,
674	    pairOptions,
675	    graphemeOptions,
676	    phonemeOptions,
677	  };
678	
679	  return (
680	    <div className="flex min-h-screen flex-col gap-4 bg-background p-4 text-foreground md:flex-row">
681	      <aside className="hidden w-full shrink-0 flex-col gap-4 md:flex md:w-80">
682	        <FiltersPanel {...filtersPanelProps} />
683	      </aside>
684	      <div className="flex items-center justify-between gap-2 md:hidden">
685	        <h1 className="text-lg font-semibold">Word Library</h1>
686	        <Sheet>
687	          <SheetTrigger asChild>
688	            <Button
689	              type="button"
690	              variant="outline"
691	              size="sm"
692	              aria-label="Open filters"
693	            >
694	              ⚙️
695	              {pills.length > 0 ? (
696	                <span className="ms-1 inline-flex size-5 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-primary text-[10px] text-primary-foreground">
697	                  {pills.length}
698	                </span>
699	              ) : null}
700	            </Button>
701	          </SheetTrigger>
702	          <SheetContent
703	            side="bottom"
704	            className="max-h-[85dvh] overflow-y-auto"
705	          >
706	            <SheetHeader>
707	              <SheetTitle>Filters</SheetTitle>
708	            </SheetHeader>
709	            <div className="p-4">
710	              <FiltersPanel {...filtersPanelProps} />
711	            </div>
712	          </SheetContent>
713	        </Sheet>
714	      </div>
715	      <main className="flex flex-1 flex-col gap-4">
716	        <div className="hidden items-center justify-between gap-2 md:flex">
717	          <h1 className="text-lg font-semibold">Word Library</h1>
718	          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
719	            {draftCount > 0 && (
720	              <button
721	                type="button"
722	                onClick={() => setShowDrafts(true)}
723	                className="text-sm underline"
724	              >
725	                Drafts ({draftCount})
726	              </button>
727	            )}
728	            <button
729	              type="button"
730	              onClick={() => setAuthoringWord('')}
731	              className="rounded bg-sky-600 px-3 py-1 text-sm text-white"
732	            >
733	              + New word
734	            </button>
735	          </div>
736	        </div>
737	        {pills.length > 0 ? (
738	          <div
739	            data-testid="active-filter-pills"
740	            className="flex flex-nowrap gap-2 overflow-x-auto py-1"
741	          >
742	            {pills.map((pill) => (
743	              <button
744	                key={pill.id}
745	                type="button"
746	                onClick={() => clearFilterPill(pill.clear)}
747	                className="inline-flex shrink-0 items-center gap-1 rounded-full bg-muted px-2.5 py-1 text-xs"
748	              >
749	                {pill.label}
750	                <span aria-hidden className="text-muted-foreground">
751	                  ✕
752	                </span>
753	                <span className="sr-only">Remove {pill.label}</span>
754	              </button>
755	            ))}
756	          </div>
757	        ) : null}
758	        {result.usedFallback ? (
759	          <Card>
760	            <CardContent className="pt-4 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
761	              Showing AUS fallback — requested region{' '}
762	              <code>{result.usedFallback.from}</code> has no entries.
763	            </CardContent>
764	          </Card>
765	        ) : null}
766	        <Pagination
767	          rangeStart={rangeStart}
768	          rangeEnd={rangeEnd}
769	          matched={matched}
770	          totalBeforeFilters={totalBeforeFilters}
771	          page={effectivePage}
772	          totalPages={totalPages}
773	          pageSize={pageSize}
774	          onPageChange={setPage}
775	          onPageSizeChange={setPageSize}
776	        />
777	        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-2 text-sm">
778	          <span className="font-medium">
779	            Showing {rangeStart}–{rangeEnd} of {matched}
780	          </span>
781	          <label className="inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-xs">
782	            <input
783	              type="checkbox"
784	              checked={chipsVisible}
785	              onChange={(e) => {
786	                setChipsUserSet(true);
787	                setChipsVisible(e.target.checked);
788	              }}
789	            />
790	            show g[p] chips
791	          </label>
792	        </div>
793	        {visible.length === 0 && wordPrefix.trim() && (
794	          <div className="rounded border border-slate-300 bg-white p-4 text-center">
795	            <p className="text-sm text-slate-600">
796	              No matches for <strong>{wordPrefix.trim()}</strong>.
797	            </p>
798	            <button
799	              type="button"
800	              onClick={() => setAuthoringWord(wordPrefix.trim())}
801	              className="mt-2 rounded bg-sky-600 px-3 py-1 text-sm text-white"
802	            >
803	              ✨ Make up this word?
804	            </button>
805	          </div>
806	        )}
807	        <div className="grid gap-4 md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3">
808	          {visible.map((hit) => (
809	            <ResultCard
810	              key={`${hit.region}-${hit.word}`}
811	              hit={hit}
812	              chipsVisible={chipsVisible}
813	            />
814	          ))}
815	        </div>
816	        <AuthoringPanel
817	          open={authoringWord !== null}
818	          initialWord={authoringWord ?? ''}
819	          onClose={() => setAuthoringWord(null)}
820	          onSaved={() => setAuthoringWord(null)}
821	        />
822	        <DraftsPanel
823	          open={showDrafts}
824	          onClose={() => setShowDrafts(false)}
825	          onEdit={(d: DraftEntry) => {
826	            setShowDrafts(false);
827	            setAuthoringWord(d.word);
828	          }}
829	        />
830	      </main>
831	    </div>
832	  );
833	};

835	interface AdvancedSectionProps {
836	  pairOptions: readonly GraphemePair[];
837	  graphemeOptions: readonly string[];
838	  phonemeOptions: readonly string[];
839	  graphemePairs: readonly GraphemePair[];
840	  onGraphemePairsChange: (next: GraphemePair[]) => void;
841	  graphemesAllowed: readonly string[];
842	  onGraphemesAllowedChange: (next: string[]) => void;
843	  graphemesRequired: readonly string[];
844	  onGraphemesRequiredChange: (next: string[]) => void;
845	  phonemesAllowed: readonly string[];
846	  onPhonemesAllowedChange: (next: string[]) => void;
847	  phonemesRequired: readonly string[];
848	  onPhonemesRequiredChange: (next: string[]) => void;
849	}
850	
851	const AdvancedSection = ({
852	  pairOptions,
853	  graphemeOptions,
854	  phonemeOptions,
855	  graphemePairs,
856	  onGraphemePairsChange,
857	  graphemesAllowed,
858	  onGraphemesAllowedChange,
859	  graphemesRequired,
860	  onGraphemesRequiredChange,
861	  phonemesAllowed,
862	  onPhonemesAllowedChange,
863	  phonemesRequired,
864	  onPhonemesRequiredChange,
865	}: AdvancedSectionProps) => (
866	  <details className="flex flex-col gap-3 rounded-lg border border-border p-3">
867	    <summary className="cursor-pointer text-sm font-medium select-none">
868	      Advanced filters
869	    </summary>
870	    <div className="mt-3 flex flex-col gap-4">
871	      <MultiSelectPairs
872	        label="Grapheme-phoneme pairs"
873	        helper="Post-filter. Hit must include every selected pair."
874	        options={pairOptions}
875	        selected={graphemePairs}
876	        onChange={onGraphemePairsChange}
877	      />
878	      <MultiSelectString

880	        helper="Hit's graphemes must all be in this set."
881	        options={graphemeOptions}

887	        helper="Hit must contain at least one of these."
888	        options={graphemeOptions}
889	        selected={graphemesRequired}
890	        onChange={onGraphemesRequiredChange}
891	      />
892	      <MultiSelectString
893	        label="Phonemes allowed"
894	        helper="Hit's phonemes must all be in this set."
895	        options={phonemeOptions}
896	        selected={phonemesAllowed}
897	        onChange={onPhonemesAllowedChange}
898	      />
899	      <MultiSelectString
900	        label="Phonemes required"
901	        helper="Hit must contain at least one of these."
902	        options={phonemeOptions}
903	        selected={phonemesRequired}
904	        onChange={onPhonemesRequiredChange}
905	      />
906	    </div>
907	  </details>
908	);

910	interface WordSearchFieldProps {
911	  value: string;
912	  onChange: (value: string) => void;
913	}
914	
915	const WordSearchField = ({ value, onChange }: WordSearchFieldProps) => {
916	  const id = useId();
917	  return (
918	    <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
919	      <Label htmlFor={id}>Word search</Label>
920	      <Input
921	        id={id}
922	        value={value}
923	        onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)}
924	        placeholder="Starts with…"
925	        autoComplete="off"
926	      />
927	    </div>
928	  );
929	};
930	
931	interface PaginationProps {
932	  rangeStart: number;
933	  rangeEnd: number;
934	  matched: number;
935	  totalBeforeFilters: number;
936	  page: number;
937	  totalPages: number;
938	  pageSize: PageSize;
939	  onPageChange: (page: number) => void;
940	  onPageSizeChange: (size: PageSize) => void;
941	}
942	
943	const Pagination = ({
944	  rangeStart,
945	  rangeEnd,
946	  matched,
947	  totalBeforeFilters,
948	  page,
949	  totalPages,
950	  pageSize,
951	  onPageChange,
952	  onPageSizeChange,
953	}: PaginationProps) => {
954	  const tokens = pageRange(page, totalPages);
955	  return (
956	    <div className="flex flex-col gap-2 md:flex-row md:items-center md:justify-between">
957	      <div className="flex items-baseline gap-2 text-sm">
958	        <span className="font-medium">
959	          Showing {rangeStart}–{rangeEnd} of {matched}
960	        </span>
961	        <span className="text-muted-foreground">
962	          (filtered from {totalBeforeFilters})
963	        </span>
964	      </div>
965	      <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2">
966	        <Button
967	          type="button"
968	          variant="outline"
969	          size="sm"
970	          disabled={page <= 1 || matched === 0}
971	          onClick={() => onPageChange(page - 1)}
972	          aria-label="Previous page"
973	        >
974	          Prev
975	        </Button>
976	        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1">
977	          {tokens.map((token) =>
978	            token === 'ellipsis-left' || token === 'ellipsis-right' ? (
979	              <span
980	                key={token}
981	                aria-hidden="true"
982	                className="px-1 text-xs text-muted-foreground"
983	              >
984	                …
985	              </span>
986	            ) : (
987	              <button
988	                key={token}
989	                type="button"
990	                aria-current={token === page ? 'page' : undefined}
991	                aria-label={`Page ${token}`}
992	                onClick={() => onPageChange(token)}
993	                className={cn(
994	                  'rounded-md border border-input px-2 py-1 text-xs transition-colors',
995	                  token === page
996	                    ? 'bg-primary text-primary-foreground'
997	                    : 'bg-transparent hover:bg-muted',
998	                )}
999	              >
1000	                {token}
1001	              </button>
1002	            ),
1003	          )}
1004	        </div>
1005	        <Button
1006	          type="button"
1007	          variant="outline"
1008	          size="sm"
1009	          disabled={page >= totalPages || matched === 0}
1010	          onClick={() => onPageChange(page + 1)}
1011	          aria-label="Next page"
1012	        >
1013	          Next
1014	        </Button>
1015	        <Select
1016	          value={String(pageSize)}
1017	          onValueChange={(v) => onPageSizeChange(Number(v) as PageSize)}
1018	        >
1019	          <SelectTrigger
1020	            className="w-[6.5rem]"
1021	            aria-label="Results per page"
1022	          >
1023	            <SelectValue />
1024	          </SelectTrigger>
1025	          <SelectContent>
1026	            {PAGE_SIZES.map((size) => (
1027	              <SelectItem key={size} value={String(size)}>
1028	                {size}/page
1029	              </SelectItem>
1030	            ))}
1031	          </SelectContent>
1032	        </Select>
1033	      </div>
1034	    </div>
1035	  );
1036	};
1037	
1038	const RegionField = () => (
1039	  <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
1040	    <Label>Region</Label>
1041	    <Select value="aus" disabled>
1042	      <SelectTrigger className="w-full">
1043	        <SelectValue />
1044	      </SelectTrigger>
1045	      <SelectContent>
1046	        {ALL_REGIONS.map((r: Region) => (
1047	          <SelectItem key={r} value={r}>
1048	            {r.toUpperCase()}
1049	          </SelectItem>
1050	        ))}
1051	      </SelectContent>
1052	    </Select>
1053	    <p className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">
1054	      UK / US / BR coming later.
1055	    </p>
1056	  </div>
1057	);
1058	
1059	interface LevelsFieldProps {
1060	  selected: readonly number[] | undefined;
1061	  onToggle: (level: number) => void;
1062	  onClear: () => void;
1063	  range: readonly [number, number] | undefined;
1064	  onRangeChange: (next?: [number, number]) => void;
1065	}
1066	
1067	const LevelsField = ({
1068	  selected,
1069	  onToggle,
1070	  onClear,
1071	  range,
1072	  onRangeChange,
1073	}: LevelsFieldProps) => {
1074	  const sel = new Set(selected);
1075	  return (
1076	    <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
1077	      <Label>Levels</Label>
1078	      <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1">
1079	        {LEVELS.map((level) => {
1080	          const active = sel.has(level);
1081	          return (
1082	            <button
1083	              key={level}
1084	              type="button"
1085	              onClick={() => onToggle(level)}
1086	              aria-pressed={active}
1087	              className={cn(
1088	                'rounded-md border border-input px-2 py-1 text-xs transition-colors',
1089	                active
1090	                  ? 'bg-primary text-primary-foreground'
1091	                  : 'bg-transparent hover:bg-muted',
1092	              )}
1093	            >
1094	              {LEVEL_LABELS.aus(level)}
1095	            </button>
1096	          );
1097	        })}
1098	      </div>
1099	      {sel.size > 0 ? (
1100	        <button
1101	          type="button"
1102	          onClick={onClear}
1103	          className="self-start text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground"
1104	        >
1105	          Clear levels
1106	        </button>
1107	      ) : null}
1108	      <p className="mt-1 text-xs text-muted-foreground">
1109	        Or set a range (ANDs with chips when both are set):
1110	      </p>
1111	      <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
1112	        <Input
1113	          type="number"
1114	          min={1}
1115	          max={8}
1116	          placeholder="min"
1117	          value={range?.[0] ?? ''}
1118	          onChange={(e) => {
1119	            if (e.target.value === '') {
1120	              onRangeChange();
1121	              return;
1122	            }
1123	            const min = Number(e.target.value);
1124	            const max = range?.[1] ?? min;
1125	            if (Number.isFinite(min)) onRangeChange([min, max]);
1126	          }}
1127	        />
1128	        <span className="text-muted-foreground">–</span>
1129	        <Input
1130	          type="number"
1131	          min={1}
1132	          max={8}
1133	          placeholder="max"
1134	          value={range?.[1] ?? ''}
1135	          onChange={(e) => {
1136	            if (e.target.value === '') {
1137	              onRangeChange();
1138	              return;
1139	            }
1140	            const max = Number(e.target.value);
1141	            const min = range?.[0] ?? max;
1142	            if (Number.isFinite(max)) onRangeChange([min, max]);
1143	          }}
1144	        />
1145	      </div>
1146	    </div>
1147	  );
1148	};
1149	
1150	interface SyllablesFieldProps {

1152	  onModeChange: (mode: SyllableMode) => void;
1153	  eq: number | undefined;
1154	  range: readonly [number, number] | undefined;
1155	  onEq: (n: number | undefined) => void;
1156	  onRange: (r: [number, number] | undefined) => void;
1157	}
1158	
1159	const SyllablesField = ({
1160	  mode,
1161	  onModeChange,
1162	  eq,
1163	  range,
1164	  onEq,
1165	  onRange,
1166	}: SyllablesFieldProps) => (
1167	  <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
1168	    <Label>Syllables</Label>
1169	    <div className="flex gap-3 text-xs">
1170	      <RadioOption
1171	        name="syllable-mode"
1172	        value="any"
1173	        current={mode}
1174	        onChange={onModeChange}
1175	        label="Any"
1176	      />
1177	      <RadioOption
1178	        name="syllable-mode"
1179	        value="eq"
1180	        current={mode}
1181	        onChange={onModeChange}
1182	        label="Exactly"
1183	      />
1184	      <RadioOption
1185	        name="syllable-mode"
1186	        value="range"
1187	        current={mode}
1188	        onChange={onModeChange}
1189	        label="Range"
1190	      />
1191	    </div>
1192	    {mode === 'eq' ? (
1193	      <Input
1194	        type="number"
1195	        min={1}
1196	        value={eq ?? ''}
1197	        onChange={(e) => {
1198	          const n =
1199	            e.target.value === '' ? undefined : Number(e.target.value);
1200	          onEq(Number.isFinite(n) ? n : undefined);
1201	        }}
1202	      />
1203	    ) : null}
1204	    {mode === 'range' ? (
1205	      <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
1206	        <Input
1207	          type="number"
1208	          min={1}
1209	          placeholder="min"
1210	          value={range?.[0] ?? ''}
1211	          onChange={(e) => {
1212	            const min = Number(e.target.value);
1213	            const max = range?.[1] ?? min;
1214	            if (Number.isFinite(min)) onRange([min, max]);
1215	          }}
1216	        />
1217	        <span className="text-muted-foreground">–</span>
1218	        <Input
1219	          type="number"
1220	          min={1}
1221	          placeholder="max"
1222	          value={range?.[1] ?? ''}
1223	          onChange={(e) => {
1224	            const max = Number(e.target.value);
1225	            const min = range?.[0] ?? max;
1226	            if (Number.isFinite(max)) onRange([min, max]);
1227	          }}
1228	        />
1229	      </div>
1230	    ) : null}
1231	  </div>
1232	);

1234	interface RadioOptionProps {

1237	  current: SyllableMode;

1239	  label: string;
1240	}
1241	
1242	const RadioOption = ({

1262	};
1263	
1264	interface FallbackFieldProps {
```

 `./.claude/worktrees/bold-sinoussi-60d7fa/src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx`

```text
1	import {
2	  useCallback,
3	  useEffect,
4	  useId,
5	  useMemo,
6	  useRef,
7	  useState,
8	} from 'react';
9	import { loadShippedIndex } from '../filter';
10	import { GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL } from '../levels';
11	import { PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA } from '../phoneme-codes';
12	import { align } from './aligner';
13	import { draftStore } from './draftStore';
14	import { generateBreakdown } from './engine';
15	import type { AlignedGrapheme } from './aligner';
16	import type { Breakdown } from './engine';
17	import type { DraftLevel } from '../types';
18	
19	const BASE_PHONEME_IPA_OPTIONS = [
20	  ...new Set(Object.values(PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA)),
21	].toSorted();
22	
23	const suggestLevel = (
24	  chips: AlignedGrapheme[],
25	): { level: DraftLevel; reason: string } => {
26	  let best: { level: DraftLevel; grapheme: string } = {
27	    level: 1,
28	    grapheme: chips[0]?.g ?? '',
29	  };
30	  for (const chip of chips) {
31	    for (const lvl of [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] as const) {
32	      const hit = (GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL[lvl] ?? []).find(
33	        (u) => u.g === chip.g,
34	      );
35	      if (hit && lvl > best.level)
36	        best = { level: lvl, grapheme: chip.g };
37	    }
38	  }
39	  return { level: best.level, reason: best.grapheme };
40	};
41	
42	export interface AuthoringPanelProps {
43	  open: boolean;
44	  onClose: () => void;
45	  initialWord: string;
46	  onSaved?: () => void;
47	}
48	
49	const DEBOUNCE_MS = 400;
50	
51	type SetChips = (
52	  value:
53	    | AlignedGrapheme[]
54	    | ((prev: AlignedGrapheme[]) => AlignedGrapheme[]),
55	) => void;
56	
57	const useDebouncedBreakdown = (
58	  word: string,
59	): {
60	  breakdown: Breakdown | null;
61	  chips: AlignedGrapheme[];
62	  setChips: SetChips;
63	  ipa: string;
64	  setIpa: (value: string) => void;
65	  level: DraftLevel;
66	  setLevel: (value: DraftLevel) => void;
67	  loading: boolean;
68	} => {
69	  const [breakdown, setBreakdown] = useState<Breakdown | null>(null);
70	  const [chips, setChips] = useState<AlignedGrapheme[]>([]);
71	  const [ipa, setIpa] = useState('');
72	  const [level, setLevel] = useState<DraftLevel>(1);
73	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
74	  // Tracks whether the user has manually edited the IPA field for the
75	  // current word.  When true, the async breakdown result must not
76	  // overwrite the user's input.  Reset to false whenever `word` changes
77	  // so that auto-fill works again for the new word.
78	  const ipaEditedRef = useRef(false);
79	
80	  const setIpaUser = useCallback((value: string) => {
81	    ipaEditedRef.current = true;
82	    setIpa(value);
83	  }, []);
84	
85	  useEffect(() => {
86	    const trimmed = word.trim();
87	    ipaEditedRef.current = false; // new word — allow auto-fill again
88	    if (!trimmed) {
89	      setBreakdown(null);
90	      setChips([]);
91	      setIpa('');
92	      setLevel(1);
93	      return;
94	    }
95	    let ignore = false;
96	    setLoading(true);
97	    const timer = setTimeout(async () => {
98	      try {
99	        const b = await generateBreakdown(trimmed);
100	        if (!ignore) {
101	          const aligned =
102	            b.ritaKnown && b.phonemes.length > 0
103	              ? align(b.word, b.phonemes)
104	              : [];
105	          setBreakdown(b);
106	          setChips(aligned);
107	          // Only auto-fill IPA from the breakdown if the user has not
108	          // manually edited the field for this word yet.
109	          if (!ipaEditedRef.current) {
110	            setIpa(b.ipa);
111	          }
112	          setLevel(
113	            aligned.length > 0 ? suggestLevel(aligned).level : 1,
114	          );
115	        }
116	      } catch (error) {
117	        // Surface engine failures (e.g. rita missing/renamed methods,
118	        // lexicon fetch errors) to devtools instead of swallowing them.
119	        console.error('[authoring] generateBreakdown failed:', error);
120	      } finally {
121	        if (!ignore) setLoading(false);
122	      }
123	    }, DEBOUNCE_MS);
124	    return () => {
125	      ignore = true;
126	      clearTimeout(timer);
127	    };
128	  }, [word]);
129	
130	  return {
131	    breakdown,
132	    chips,
133	    setChips,
134	    ipa,
135	    setIpa: setIpaUser,
136	    level,
137	    setLevel,
138	    loading,
139	  };
140	};
141	
142	export const AuthoringPanel = ({
143	  open,
144	  onClose,
145	  initialWord,
146	  onSaved,
147	}: AuthoringPanelProps) => {
148	  const titleId = useId();
149	  const wordInputId = useId();
150	  const dialogRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement | null>(null);
151	  const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement | null>(null);
152	
153	  const [word, setWord] = useState(initialWord);
154	  const { breakdown, chips, setChips, ipa, setIpa, level, setLevel } =
155	    useDebouncedBreakdown(word);
156	
157	  const [editingIndex, setEditingIndex] = useState<number | null>(null);
158	  const [variantsInput, setVariantsInput] = useState('');
159	  const [shippedSet, setShippedSet] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());
160	  const [dirty, setDirty] = useState(false);
161	  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);
162	
163	  useEffect(() => {
164	    if (!open) return;
165	    let ignore = false;
166	    void loadShippedIndex('aus').then((set) => {
167	      if (!ignore) setShippedSet(set);
168	    });
169	    return () => {
170	      ignore = true;
171	    };
172	  }, [open]);
173	
174	  const syllables = breakdown?.syllables ?? [];
175	  const suggestion = chips.length > 0 ? suggestLevel(chips) : null;
176	
177	  const phonemeIpaOptions = useMemo(
178	    () =>
179	      [
180	        ...new Set([
181	          ...BASE_PHONEME_IPA_OPTIONS,
182	          ...chips.map((c) => c.p),
183	        ]),
184	      ].toSorted(),
185	    [chips],
186	  );
187	
188	  const updateChip = (i: number, patch: Partial<AlignedGrapheme>) => {
189	    setChips((prev) => {
190	      const next = [...prev];
191	      const existing = next[i];
192	      if (!existing) return prev;
193	      next[i] = {
194	        ...existing,
195	        ...patch,
196	        confidence: patch.confidence ?? existing.confidence,
197	      };
198	      return next;
199	    });
200	    setDirty(true);
201	  };
202	
203	  const extendChip = (i: number) => {
204	    setChips((prev) => {
205	      const next = [...prev];
206	      const current = next[i];
207	      if (!current) return prev;
208	      const nextChip = next[i + 1];
209	      if (!nextChip || nextChip.g.length < 2) return prev;
210	      next[i] = { ...current, g: current.g + nextChip.g[0]! };
211	      next[i + 1] = { ...nextChip, g: nextChip.g.slice(1) };
212	      return next;
213	    });
214	    setDirty(true);
215	  };
216	
217	  const shrinkChip = (i: number) => {
218	    setChips((prev) => {
219	      const next = [...prev];
220	      const current = next[i];
221	      if (!current || current.g.length < 2) return prev;
222	      const nextChip = next[i + 1];
223	      if (!nextChip) return prev;
224	      const moved = current.g.slice(-1);
225	      next[i] = { ...current, g: current.g.slice(0, -1) };
226	      next[i + 1] = { ...nextChip, g: moved + nextChip.g };
227	      return next;
228	    });
229	    setDirty(true);
230	  };
231	
232	  const handleSave = useCallback(async () => {
233	    const trimmed = word.trim().toLowerCase();
234	    const variants = variantsInput
235	      .split(',')
236	      .map((s) => s.trim())
237	      .filter(Boolean);
238	    setSaving(true);
239	    try {
240	      await draftStore.saveDraft({
241	        word: trimmed,
242	        region: 'aus',
243	        level,
244	        ipa,
245	        syllables: breakdown?.syllables ?? [trimmed],
246	        syllableCount: (breakdown?.syllables ?? [trimmed]).length,
247	        graphemes: chips.map(({ confidence: _c, ...rest }) => rest),
248	        ...(variants.length > 0 ? { variants } : {}),
249	        ritaKnown: breakdown?.ritaKnown ?? false,
250	      });
251	      onSaved?.();
252	      onClose();
253	    } finally {
254	      setSaving(false);
255	    }
256	  }, [
257	    word,
258	    variantsInput,
259	    level,
260	    ipa,
261	    breakdown,
262	    chips,
263	    onSaved,
264	    onClose,
265	  ]);
266	
267	  const closeWithConfirm = useCallback(() => {
268	    if (dirty && !globalThis.confirm('Discard this draft?')) return;
269	    onClose();
270	  }, [dirty, onClose]);
271	
272	  const handleKey = useCallback(
273	    (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
274	      if (e.key === 'Escape') closeWithConfirm();
275	    },
276	    [closeWithConfirm],
277	  );
278	
279	  useEffect(() => {
280	    if (!open) return;
281	    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', handleKey);
282	    return () => globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKey);
283	  }, [open, handleKey]);
284	
285	  useEffect(() => {
286	    if (open) {
287	      dialogRef.current?.focus();
288	      inputRef.current?.focus();
289	    }
290	  }, [open]);
291	
292	  if (!open) return null;
293	
294	  return (
295	    <div
296	      ref={dialogRef}
297	      role="dialog"
298	      aria-modal="true"
299	      aria-labelledby={titleId}
300	      tabIndex={-1}
301	      className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/40 p-0 md:p-6"
302	    >
303	      <div className="flex h-full w-full flex-col bg-white p-6 md:h-auto md:max-w-2xl md:rounded-xl md:shadow-2xl">
304	        <h2 id={titleId} className="text-xl font-semibold">
305	          Make up a word
306	        </h2>
307	        <p className="mt-1 text-sm text-slate-600">
308	          Authoring <strong>{initialWord || '(new word)'}</strong>.
309	          Fields below populate as you type.
310	        </p>
311	        <div className="mt-4 flex-1 space-y-4 overflow-auto">
312	          <div className="flex flex-col gap-1">
313	            <label
314	              htmlFor={wordInputId}
315	              className="text-sm font-medium"
316	            >
317	              Word
318	            </label>
319	            <input
320	              ref={inputRef}
321	              id={wordInputId}
322	              type="text"
323	              value={word}
324	              onChange={(e) => {
325	                setWord(e.target.value);
326	                setEditingIndex(null);
327	                setDirty(true);
328	              }}
329	              className="rounded border px-3 py-2"
330	            />
331	          </div>
332	          {breakdown && !breakdown.ritaKnown && word.trim() && (
333	            <div className="rounded border border-amber-300 bg-amber-50 p-3 text-sm">
334	              RitaJS doesn&apos;t know <strong>{word.trim()}</strong>.
335	              Look it up →{' '}
336	              <a
337	                href={`https://www.dictionary.com/browse/${encodeURIComponent(word.trim())}`}
338	                target="_blank"
339	                rel="noreferrer noopener"
340	                className="underline"
341	              >
342	                Open in dictionary.com
343	              </a>
344	            </div>
345	          )}
346	          {chips.length > 0 && (
347	            <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
348	              {chips.map((chip, i) => (
349	                <button
350	                  // eslint-disable-next-line react/no-array-index-key -- chips re-derive wholesale from align() per word; positional key is stable enough
351	                  key={`${i}-${chip.g}`}

353	                  data-testid="grapheme-chip"
354	                  aria-invalid={
355	                    chip.confidence < 0.5 ? 'true' : undefined
356	                  }
357	                  className={`rounded border px-2 py-1 text-sm ${
358	                    chip.confidence < 0.5
359	                      ? 'border-amber-400 bg-amber-50'
360	                      : 'border-slate-300 bg-white'
361	                  }`}
362	                  onClick={() => setEditingIndex(i)}
363	                >
364	                  <div className="font-semibold">{chip.g}</div>
365	                  <div className="text-xs text-slate-500">
366	                    /{chip.p}/
367	                  </div>
368	                </button>
369	              ))}
370	            </div>
371	          )}
372	          {editingIndex !== null && chips[editingIndex] && (
373	            <div className="rounded border border-slate-300 bg-slate-50 p-3">
374	              <label className="flex flex-col gap-1 text-sm">
375	                Phoneme
376	                <select
377	                  aria-label="phoneme"
378	                  value={chips[editingIndex].p}

382	                  className="rounded border px-2 py-1"
383	                >

387	                    </option>

392	                <button

419	          <label className="flex flex-col gap-1 text-sm font-medium">

422	              type="text"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 01:52:24Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit the fix

```bash
git add src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.authoring.test.tsx src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.tsx src/data/words/filter.test.ts src/data/words/filter.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(word-authoring): level-aware empty-state when a shipped word is filtered out

When a user's search matches a word that exists in shipped data but is
hidden by the current filter (e.g. filtering to Level 1 while searching
"adore", which is Level 8), the previous "✨ Make up this word?" CTA
was misleading — opening the authoring panel then surfaced a dead-end
"already exists — open that entry instead" message even though the
shipped entry was invisible behind the filter.

The empty-state now branches on a new `loadShippedWordLevels(region)`
map: if the search term is shipped at any level, we render "<word> is
available at Level N, but your current filters hide it." with a
"Clear filters" button that resets every filter except region,
fallback, and the word-prefix search, so the previously-hidden match
immediately appears in the grid.

- TDD: failing tests for `loadShippedWordLevels` (word → lowest-level
  map) and for the new empty-state paths ("available at Level N",
  clear-filters restores the hit) driving the implementation.
- `clearAllFilters` also resets syllable-mode + grapheme-pair state so
  those fields don't silently re-filter after the reset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20
```

```bash
git add src/data/words/filter.ts src/data/words/filter.test.ts src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.tsx src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.authoring.test.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(word-authoring): empty-state copy stops blaming the level filter when it isn't the cause

When the shipped word's level is already inside the active level
filter (e.g. level=8 + syllableCountEq=1 + search "adore"), the
previous "available at Level 8, but your current filters hide it"
wording implied Level 8 was the blocker — it wasn't. The syllable
filter was.

The empty-state now routes through a new `isLevelInFilter(filter,
level)` helper: when the level IS in the filter, the message drops
the level mention and reads "<word> exists in shipped data, but your
current filters hide it." The level-mention branch is preserved for
the original case (level filter excludes the word) where naming the
level helps the user understand what to clear.

- TDD: failing test that filters to Level 8 + syllables=1, searches
  "adore", and asserts the message omits "Level 8".
- Unit tests for `isLevelInFilter` covering level/levels[]/levelRange.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10
```

```text
8	class DraftDB extends Dexie {
9	  drafts!: Table<DraftEntry, string>;
10	
11	  constructor() {
12	    super(DB_NAME);

29	const nowIso = (): string => new Date().toISOString();
30	
31	const omitId = (d: DraftEntry): ExportedDraft => {
32	  const { id: _id, ...rest } = d;
33	  return rest;
34	};
35	
36	export const draftStore = {
37	  async saveDraft(
38	    input: Omit<DraftEntry, 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>,
39	  ): Promise<DraftEntry> {
40	    const existing = await db.drafts
41	      .where('[region+word]')
42	      .equals([input.region, input.word])
43	      .first();
44	    if (existing) {
45	      throw new Error(
46	        `draft for "${input.word}" (${input.region}) already exists`,
47	      );
48	    }
49	    const now = nowIso();
50	    const entry: DraftEntry = {
51	      id: nanoid(),
52	      ...input,
53	      createdAt: now,
54	      updatedAt: now,
55	    };
56	    await db.drafts.add(entry);
57	    return entry;
58	  },
59	
60	  async updateDraft(
61	    id: string,
62	    patch: Partial<
63	      Omit<DraftEntry, 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'region' | 'word'>
64	    >,
65	  ): Promise<DraftEntry> {
66	    const current = await db.drafts.get(id);
67	    if (!current) throw new Error(`draft not found: ${id}`);
68	    const next: DraftEntry = {
69	      ...current,
70	      ...patch,
71	      updatedAt: nowIso(),
72	    };
73	    await db.drafts.put(next);
74	    return next;
75	  },
76	
77	  async deleteDraft(id: string): Promise<void> {
78	    await db.drafts.delete(id);
79	  },
80	
81	  async listDrafts(opts: { region: Region }): Promise<DraftEntry[]> {
82	    return db.drafts
83	      .where('[region+word]')
84	      .between([opts.region, ''], [opts.region, '￿'])
85	      .toArray();
86	  },
87	
88	  async exportDrafts(): Promise<DraftsExport> {
89	    const all = await db.drafts.toArray();
90	    return {
91	      version: 1,
92	      exportedAt: nowIso(),
93	      drafts: all.map((d) => omitId(d)),
94	    };
```

```text
11	const triggerDownload = (blob: Blob, filename: string) => {
12	  const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
13	  const link = document.createElement('a');
14	  link.href = url;
15	  link.download = filename;
16	  document.body.append(link);
17	  link.click();

25	    type: 'application/json',
26	  });
27	  const iso = new Date().toISOString().replaceAll(/[:.]/g, '-');
28	  triggerDownload(blob, `wordlib-export-${iso}.json`);
29	};
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 02:00:38Z)**_

Both bugs share one root cause: the Edit flow passes only `d.word`, the panel re-derives from scratch, and then save hits the "new draft" path which throws because a draft already exists.

Plan:
1. `WordLibraryExplorer`: pass the whole `DraftEntry` to `AuthoringPanel` when editing (new prop `initialDraft`), plus a `key` so the panel remounts cleanly per draft.
2. `AuthoringPanel`: seed all fields from the draft when `initialDraft` is set; save path routes through `updateDraft` instead of `saveDraft`.
3. TDD both.

Commit edit-draft fix

```bash
git add src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.tsx src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.test.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(word-authoring): editing a draft pre-fills the form and saves in place

Two related bugs surfaced together when clicking Edit on an existing
draft: the authoring panel opened with every field blank (only the
draft's word was wired through — IPA, chips, level, syllables, and
variants were re-derived from rita and overwrote nothing because rita
doesn't know the word), and then clicking Save threw "draft for
\"prothesis\" (aus) already exists" because saveDraft was called on
the existing row.

Root cause: the Edit flow only passed `d.word` to the panel, and the
panel had no notion of edit-vs-new mode.

- `AuthoringPanel` now accepts `initialDraft?: DraftEntry`. When set,
  every input seeds from the draft (word, IPA, level, graphemes →
  chips with confidence=1, syllables, variants) and the debounced
  breakdown effect skips its first run so rita can't clobber the
  seeded state on mount. Subsequent word-field edits still trigger
  re-derivation as before.
- Save routes through `draftStore.updateDraft(id, patch)` when
  `initialDraft` is set, avoiding the uniqueness guard on
  `[region+word]`. New-word flow is unchanged.
- `WordLibraryExplorer` holds a separate `editingDraft` state and
  keys the panel by `edit-<id>` / `new-<word>` so switching between
  drafts remounts the panel with fresh internal state rather than
  reusing the previous debounced-hook instance.

TDD: new AuthoringPanel tests cover the pre-fill (word/IPA/level/
chip count/syllable chips) and the in-place update (single row,
same id, tweaked level persists) — both failed before the change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10
```

```text
{"body":"## Problem\n\nPlans produced by planning agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) can prescribe code shapes that conflict with project conventions encoded in `.claude/skills/`. Executor agents then follow the plan verbatim because the plan is treated as source of truth.\n\nConcrete incident surfacing this: PR #182 shipped four named Storybook stories per component (\\`Default\\`, \\`UnknownWord\\`, \\`DuplicateOfShipped\\`, \\`LowConfidenceChips\\`) in Task 15 of the Word Authoring plan, because the plan's template prescribed that exact shape. Our \\`write-storybook\\` skill enforces a single-Default \\\"Playground\\\" pattern instead. Something concise:\n\n> ### Before writing a plan\n>\n> Plans are binding source of truth for executor agents. If a plan will create or modify any of the following, invoke the corresponding project skill **before** prescribing code, and let the skill override plan boilerplate:\n>\n> | Plan touches                                | Required skill                  |\n> | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n> | \\`*.stories.tsx\\`                             | \\`write-storybook\\`               |\n> | \\`tests-vr/**\\`, \\`tests-e2e/**\\`, \\`*.e2e.ts\\` | \\`write-e2e-vr-tests\\`            |\n> | \\`*.md\\` authored by an agent                 | existing Markdown Authoring rules in this file |\n> | Architecture docs (\\`*.mdx\\` under game state)| \\`update-architecture-docs\\`      |\n>\n> If a plan's code sample conflicts with a skill, flag it as a \\\"Spec Delta\\\" at the top of the plan and justify the deviation — don't silently ship boilerplate that contradicts project convention.\n\nExecutors should mirror this: when the plan prescribes files matching the table above, load the skill into the implementer's context as the authoritative reference.\n\n## Scope\n\n- [ ] Add the clause to \\`CLAUDE.md\\` under a new \\\"Before writing a plan\\\" section (after the React Best Practices section, before Git Workflow, so it's early in the file)\n- [ ] Mirror the clause into \\`AGENTS.md\\` so Cursor picks it up (AGENTS.md currently delegates to CLAUDE.md for full rules — decide whether to duplicate the section or move it into AGENTS.md and have CLAUDE.md reference it)\n- [ ] Optional: add a \\\"For plan authors\\\" preamble to each of the named skills (belt-and-suspenders) — \\`write-storybook\\` is the obvious first candidate\n\n## Out of scope\n\nRefactoring the PR #182 stories to match — tracked in a separate issue.\n\n## Success criteria\n\nNext plan that touches any of the file patterns above arrives with an explicit reference to the corresponding skill, or a Spec Delta table noting the deviation. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 02:18:18Z)**_
```

No existing compound-word issue. I'll draft one following the repo's Problem/Proposal/Scope/Out of scope/Success criteria style.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 02:18:55Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Create compound-words feature issue

```bash
gh issue create --title "feat(word-authoring): compose compound words from existing parts (sun + day → sunday)" --label enhancement --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

The Word Library only surfaces words that have been authored individually — either in the shipped curriculum or as a draft. Many common English words are transparent compounds of words we already ship (e.g. `sunday` = `sun` + `day`, `snowman` = `snow` + `man`, `football` = `foot` + `ball`). A teacher who needs `sunday` today has two bad options: author the whole word from scratch in the authoring panel (re-keying IPA, re-picking chips for letters the curriculum already covers), or skip it.

Both bad options throw away information we already have: the parts carry shipped-quality grapheme chips, IPA, syllables, and level metadata. A compound-builder lets us reuse all of that.

Surfaced while manually verifying the fixes in PR #182 / handoff `2026-04-24-word-authoring-pr182-ux-gaps.md`.

## Proposal

Add a compound-authoring flow that lets the user pick 2+ existing `WordHit`s (shipped or draft) in order, and seed the authoring panel with the concatenation. The existing edit-draft wiring added in commit `8ecf13ac` already accepts a pre-filled `initialDraft`, so the compound flow can reuse that plumbing.

### Composition rules (MVP)

Given ordered parts `[sun, day]`:

| Field           | Rule                                                            |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `word`          | `parts.map(p => p.word).join('')`                               |
| `graphemes`     | `parts.flatMap(p => p.graphemes)` (requires all parts to have chips — Tier-2+ only for MVP) |
| `syllables`     | `parts.flatMap(p => p.syllables ?? [p.word])`                   |
| `syllableCount` | sum                                                             |
| `ipa`           | `parts.map(p => p.ipa ?? '').join('')` (concat, no slashes — matches current convention; user polishes stress marks manually) |
| `level`         | `Math.max(...parts.map(p => p.level))` (highest-grapheme-used proxy) |
| `ritaKnown`     | `await RiTa.hasWord(compound)` — used to decide whether to show the \"rita doesn't know this word\" banner |

### UX entry points

1. **Explorer header.** Add a `⛓ Combine words` button next to `+ New word` / `Drafts (N)`. Opens a compound picker dialog.
2. **Empty-state suggestion (stretch).** When the user's search returns zero results and the term decomposes into shipped words (`scanShippedForDecomposition(term, shippedLevels)`), show `Try combining: sun + day →` alongside the existing CTAs.

### Compound picker dialog

- Ordered list of slots (start with 2; `+ Add part` to append; remove / reorder with up/down buttons).
- Each slot is a prefix-search combobox over shipped+draft words in the current region.
- Live preview row: `sun + day` → **sunday** — IPA `sʌndeɪ` — L2 (from day).
- `Continue → Author` opens `AuthoringPanel` with `initialDraft` seeded from the composition (no `id` yet — save path creates a new draft via the existing `saveDraft` branch).
- `Cancel` closes without touching state.

## Scope

- [ ] Pure helper `composeCompound(parts: WordHit[]): ComposeCompoundResult` with unit tests (ordering, chip concat, level max, empty/missing-chip edge cases, rita lookup)
- [ ] `CompoundPicker` component + story + unit tests (prefix search, add/remove/reorder slots, live preview)
- [ ] Explorer entry point button + wiring to the picker + handoff to `AuthoringPanel`
- [ ] Extend `AuthoringPanel`'s seed path to accept a \"new but pre-filled\" shape (reuse `initialDraft` structure but without `id`; save via `saveDraft` not `updateDraft`)
- [ ] Storybook coverage for the picker and the seeded `AuthoringPanel`
- [ ] Update the handoff / this issue with links from PR #182 for visibility

## Out of scope

- **Auto-decomposition suggestions in the empty-state.** Worth doing, but it needs a decomposition scanner and ranking heuristics (`snowman` → `snow + man` vs `s + now + man`) — track separately after MVP ships.
- **Stress-shift heuristics.** Real compounds often retract stress (`ˈblackbird` ≠ black + bird concat). MVP concatenates literal IPA and leaves stress marks to manual polish; the authoring panel already exposes the IPA field for that.
- **Hyphenated compounds** (`ice-cream`, `sister-in-law`). Allow a `separator: '' | '-' | ' '` option in the helper for future use, but the UI in MVP only offers blank-concat.
- **Affix-only parts** (`un-`, `-less`). Affixes aren't standalone entries in the word library; supporting them requires a separate affix store.
- **Tier-1-only parts** (shipped words without `graphemes`). Surface a friendly error in the picker and block `Continue` until all parts are Tier-2+.

## Success criteria

- User can compose `sunday` from `sun` + `day` in ≤ 15 seconds without typing any IPA or picking any chips manually; the saved draft's chip array equals `sun.graphemes ++ day.graphemes` byte-for-byte.
- `composeCompound` round-trips through `yarn words:import` (the CLI added in commit `30524cc9`) without schema errors for any 2-part or 3-part compound of shipped Tier-2+ words.
- Picker rejects Tier-1-only parts with a clear error before `Continue` is enabled.
- Compound drafts appear in the Drafts panel exactly like manually-authored drafts (edit flow already works thanks to `8ecf13ac`).

## Related

- PR #182 — word authoring baseline that this feature builds on
- Handoff: `.claude/handoffs/2026-04-24-word-authoring-pr182-ux-gaps.md` — source of this discovery
- #183 — before this plan is written, author must consult `write-storybook` + TDD skills (the Storybook work here lands in one component, so the single-Playground pattern applies)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
```

```typescript
import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
import { normalizeIpa } from './ipa';

describe('normalizeIpa', () => {
  it('returns an already-clean value untouched', () => {
    expect(normalizeIpa('pʊtɪŋ')).toBe('pʊtɪŋ');
  });

  it('strips wrapping slashes', () => {
    expect(normalizeIpa('/pʊtɪŋ/')).toBe('pʊtɪŋ');
  });

  it('strips slashes anywhere in the string', () => {
    expect(normalizeIpa('/pʊ/tɪŋ/')).toBe('pʊtɪŋ');
  });

  it('strips all whitespace including non-breaking spaces', () => {
    expect(normalizeIpa(' / pʊtɪŋ / ')).toBe('pʊtɪŋ');
    expect(normalizeIpa('pʊt\tɪŋ')).toBe('pʊtɪŋ');
    expect(normalizeIpa('pʊt ɪŋ')).toBe('pʊtɪŋ');
  });

  it('preserves stress marks (ˈ, ˌ) and length marks (ː)', () => {
    expect(normalizeIpa('/ˈprɒθɪsɪs/')).toBe('ˈprɒθɪsɪs');
    expect(normalizeIpa('/ˌɪnfəˈmeɪʃən/')).toBe('ˌɪnfəˈmeɪʃən');
    expect(normalizeIpa('/biːt/')).toBe('biːt');
  });

  it('returns empty string for empty or whitespace-only input', () => {
    expect(normalizeIpa('')).toBe('');
    expect(normalizeIpa('   ')).toBe('');
    expect(normalizeIpa('//')).toBe('');
  });
});

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
/**
 * Normalises an IPA string to the shipped-curriculum convention:
 * concatenated symbols with no slash wrappers and no whitespace.
 * Stress marks (ˈ, ˌ) and length marks (ː) are preserved — they
 * appear in real IPA transcriptions and teachers may want them.
 *
 * The fix-up matters at two boundaries:
 *   1. Save — users who paste from dictionaries land values like
 *      "/ˈprɒθɪsɪs/". Persisting that would make every downstream
 *      consumer that wraps ipa in /.../ render "//ˈprɒθɪsɪs//".
 *   2. Display — defensive: any legacy draft that snuck in with
 *      slashes still renders cleanly after we strip at read time.
 */
export const normalizeIpa = (value: string): string =>
  value.replaceAll('/', '').replaceAll(/\s+/g, '');

```

Clear answer from the shipped data:

| stress `ˈ` / `ˌ` | ❌ zero matches | **strip** |
| length `ː` | ✅ yes (`biːt`, `æŋriː`) | **keep** |

So: strip slashes, whitespace, and stress. Keep length marks. Let me update the helper + tests accordingly.

Commit IPA normalization

```bash
git add src/data/words/authoring/ipa.ts src/data/words/authoring/ipa.test.ts src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.test.tsx src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.tsx src/data/words/WordLibraryExplorer.card.test.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(word-authoring): normalise IPA to the shipped-curriculum shape on save + render

Pasting IPA from a dictionary (e.g. "/ˈprɒθɪsɪs/") into the authoring
panel persisted verbatim and then rendered as "//ˈprɒθɪsɪs//" in the
result card, because ResultCard wraps hit.ipa in /.../ for display.
The shipped curriculum JSONs use a single convention: concatenated
symbols, no slashes, no whitespace, no stress marks — length marks
(ː) are the only non-phoneme characters that appear, and they matter
phonetically (biːt ≠ bɪt).

- `normalizeIpa(value)` strips `/`, all whitespace, and primary/
  secondary stress marks (ˈ, ˌ); length marks are kept. Convention
  was verified by scanning every shipped AUS level JSON: zero
  slashes, zero whitespace, zero stress; length marks present in
  level7.json.
- Applied at the save boundary in `AuthoringPanel.handleSave` so
  drafts land in the same shape as shipped data (and round-trip
  cleanly through the words:import CLI).
- Applied defensively at the display boundary in `ResultCard` so any
  legacy draft that predates this fix still renders without
  double-slashes.
- The Save button's disabled guard now checks the normalised value,
  not the raw input — otherwise a user could "pass" the non-empty
  check with "//" and then save an empty string.

TDD: 7 unit tests for `normalizeIpa` (wrapped slashes, interior
slashes, whitespace incl. tabs, stress strip, length preserve,
empty-ish inputs). Integration: AuthoringPanel test types
"/ˈzwɜːd/" and asserts the persisted draft's ipa is "zwɜːd";
ResultCard test renders a hit with "/ ˈpʊtɪŋ /" and asserts the
button text is "/pʊtɪŋ/" without doubled slashes or stress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 02:32:33Z)**_

Committed locally as `0c0d9463`, no push.

**On your spaces-and-stress question, the shipped curriculum answers it:** I scanned every AUS JSON.

| Mark | Count in shipped | Decision |
|---|---|---|
| `/` slashes | 0 | strip |
| whitespace | 0 | strip |
| stress `ˈ` / `ˌ` | **0** | strip |
| length `ː` | present (`biːt`, `æŋriː`) | **keep** (phonemically load-bearing) |

So the helper strips slashes, whitespace, and stress marks only. Length marks pass through.

What to verify in-browser:

- Edit a draft, paste `/ˈprɒθɪsɪs/` into IPA, Save → reopen → field shows `prɒθɪsɪs`; result card shows `/prɒθɪsɪs/` (single slashes).
- Any shipped word like `biːt` still renders `/biːt/` (length mark preserved).
- A user who types only `//` or `ˈ ` into IPA → Save stays disabled (empty after normalisation).

Branch: 8 ahead of origin. Ready for the next one.

---

```bash
feat(word-authoring): chip editor gains delete, a selection ring, and an on-screen hint

Three small UX additions to the authoring panel's chip row, bundled
together because they only make sense once you can see the thing
you're editing:

- Delete (🗑): the chip editor popover now has a Delete button that
  removes the selected chip. The removed letters re-attach to the
  previous chip (or the next chip when deleting index 0), so the
  invariant `chips.map(c => c.g).join('') === word` still holds.
  Disabled when only one chip remains (nothing to merge into). Added
  alongside the existing shrink/extend controls.
- Selection ring: the chip the user just clicked now carries
  `ring-2 ring-sky-500 ring-offset-1` and `aria-pressed="true"`.
  Previously there was no visual tie between which chip you clicked
  and the editor popover below, which made the shrink/extend/delete
  affordances confusing on longer words (`putting`, `prothesis`).
  The amber low-confidence treatment still layers underneath.
- Hint line above the chip row explains the three things chips can
  do ("Click a chip to change its phoneme, rebalance letters, or
  delete it…"). Cheap discoverability; no a11y harm since the
  controls themselves remain the source of truth.

TDD: hint text, ring class, delete-merges-into-previous,
delete-first-merges-into-next, delete-disabled-for-single-chip — all
failed before the implementation. `chips.map(c => c.g).join('') ===
word` is asserted in both delete tests.

Part of the handoff UX bundle in
`.claude/handoffs/2026-04-24-word-authoring-pr182-ux-gaps.md` (items:
deleteChip helper, selected-chip ring, instructional line).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

_**User (2026-04-24 02:56:36Z)**_

I can delete a chip, but I can't add any? Is it hard to include that?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 02:57:17Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 02:57:19Z)**_

Not hard — the natural complement to Delete is **Split**: take the last letter of the selected chip and make it its own chip. It's the only operation that can add a chip (since chips must cover every letter of the word, you can't add "new" letters, you can only carve an existing chip in two).

TDD the same way.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>

Commit Split feature

```bash
git add src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.test.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(word-authoring): chip editor gains ✂ Split — the inverse of Delete

Users couldn't add a chip, only delete or rebalance — but "add" isn't
a well-defined operation when `chips.map(c => c.g).join('') === word`
must hold, because any new chip has to get its letters from somewhere.
Split expresses that directly: take the trailing letter of the selected
chip and carve it into a new chip inserted at `i + 1`. The new chip
inherits the source chip's phoneme as a placeholder and is marked
low-confidence (confidence: 0.4) so the amber chip treatment cues the
user to re-check the phoneme.

- `splitChip(i)` in AuthoringPanel.tsx. Disabled when the chip has a
  single letter (nothing to split off).
- New ✂ Split button in the chip editor popover, between Extend (+) and
  Delete (🗑). Focus jumps to the newly-created chip (`i + 1`) so the
  phoneme dropdown is already targeting it.
- Instructional hint updated to list all four actions so the button
  bar isn't self-documenting alone.

TDD: split-preserves-join (count goes up by one; concatenation still
equals the word; uses findIndex to locate a multi-letter chip rather
than hardcoding aligner output), and split-disabled-for-single-letter.

Completes the "can-do-anything-to-a-chip" surface: change phoneme,
shrink (−), extend (+), split (✂), delete (🗑).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -6
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit bootstrap chip

```bash
git add src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.test.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(word-authoring): chip row seeds a bootstrap chip for rita-unknown words

Before: typing a word RitaJS doesn't know (e.g. "prothesis") left the
chip row empty because alignment needs phonemes. With no chips, the
Split button had nothing to operate on, so the user was stuck — the
only way to author the word's graphemes was to type each of them…
but there was no UI for that.

After: when the engine returns an empty alignment but the word has
text, `useDebouncedBreakdown` seeds a single chip `{g: word, p: '',
confidence: 0.2}`. The chip shows amber (low-confidence) to cue the
user to edit it, and ✂ Split is immediately enabled (the bootstrap
chip has length >= 2 for any real word). Repeated splits carve the
word into graphemes letter by letter; each split inherits the source
phoneme as a placeholder, which the user can fix via the phoneme
dropdown. Invariant `chips.map(c => c.g).join('') === word` still
holds by construction.

The edit-draft path is unaffected — seeded drafts already land chips
via `seedFromDraft`, and the hook's `skipNextRef` guard keeps the
first breakdown run from overwriting them.

TDD: failing test "bootstraps a single full-word chip when rita does
not know the word, so split works" renders with initialWord=
"prothesis", asserts exactly one amber chip with the full word, and
checks that the Split button is enabled after selecting it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
```

```bash
feat(word-authoring): play-phoneme button next to the Phoneme label

The chip editor let you pick a phoneme from a dropdown but gave no way
to hear what you just picked. A teacher verifying that "ng" in putting
really maps to /ŋ/ (not /n/ + /g/) had no audible check in the panel
— they had to save, close, and click the phoneme-audio chips in the
result card.

Add an inline speaker button directly next to the "Phoneme" label in
the chip editor:

- Label: `Phoneme  [🔈 /ŋ/]` — mirrors the existing IPA-play button
  pattern in ResultCard (same 🔈 glyph, same `/p/` wrapping, same
  font-mono style token).
- Accessible name: `Play phoneme /ŋ/` so screen readers announce the
  action and the specific phoneme.
- Click calls `playPhoneme(chip.p)` via the existing phoneme-audio
  module — no new audio plumbing.
- Disabled (opacity-50, proper `disabled`) when the chip has no
  phoneme yet, which is the case for bootstrap chips on rita-unknown
  words before the user picks one from the dropdown.

TDD: two failing tests — one asserts the button exists with the
phoneme in its accessible name and text after selecting the first
chip of "putting"; the other asserts it's disabled for the bootstrap
chip of a rita-unknown word ("prothesis").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
```

```text
1	export const ARPABET_TO_IPA: Readonly<Record<string, string>> = {
2	  aa: 'ɑ',
3	  ae: 'æ',
4	  ah: 'ʌ',
5	  ao: 'ɔ',
6	  aw: 'aʊ',
7	  ay: 'aɪ',
8	  b: 'b',
9	  ch: 'tʃ',
10	  d: 'd',
11	  dh: 'ð',
12	  eh: 'ɛ',
13	  er: 'ɜ',
14	  ey: 'eɪ',
15	  f: 'f',
16	  g: 'g',
17	  hh: 'h',
18	  ih: 'ɪ',
19	  iy: 'i',
20	  jh: 'dʒ',
21	  k: 'k',
22	  l: 'l',
23	  m: 'm',
24	  n: 'n',
25	  ng: 'ŋ',
26	  ow: 'oʊ',
27	  oy: 'ɔɪ',
28	  p: 'p',
29	  r: 'r',
30	  s: 's',
31	  sh: 'ʃ',
32	  t: 't',
33	  th: 'θ',
34	  uh: 'ʊ',
35	  uw: 'u',
36	  v: 'v',
37	  w: 'w',
38	  y: 'j',
39	  z: 'z',
40	  zh: 'ʒ',
41	};
42	
43	const STRESS_SUFFIX = /[0-9]+$/;
44	
45	export const arpabetTokenToIpa = (token: string): string => {
46	  const stripped = token
47	    .trim()
48	    .toLowerCase()
49	    .replace(STRESS_SUFFIX, '');
50	  const ipa = ARPABET_TO_IPA[stripped];
51	  if (!ipa) {
52	    throw new Error(`unknown ARPABET token: ${token}`);
53	  }
54	  return ipa;
55	};
56	
57	export const arpabetStringToIpa = (input: string): string[] => {
58	  if (!input.trim()) return [];
59	  return input
60	    .trim()
61	    .split(/\s+/)
62	    .flatMap((syll) => syll.split('-').filter(Boolean))
63	    .map((token) => arpabetTokenToIpa(token));
64	};
65
```

```bash
git add src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.tsx src/data/words/authoring/AuthoringPanel.test.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(word-authoring): phoneme dropdown offers the full AUS inventory, not a 9-entry shortcut

The Phoneme <select> in the chip editor was pulling its options from
`PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA`, which is a 9-entry map of teacher-friendly
codes for irregular English spellings (sh → ʃ, ch → tʃ, ng → ŋ, …).
That map was never meant to be the phoneme inventory, but the panel
treated it as such, so authoring could only ever choose from 9
phonemes — no ɒ, no ɜː, no iː, no ae/aɪ/aʊ/eɪ, etc. Unusable for
anything but the narrowest re-labelling.

Primary source is now the 44-unit teaching inventory in
`GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL` (the canonical AUS curriculum progression).
We still union `PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA`'s values so the bare schwa `ə`
stays available — the shipped curriculum only teaches it inside
diphthongs (`eə`, `ɛə`, `ɪə`), but teachers will want it available
for unstressed vowels in longer words.

- 44 curriculum phonemes ∪ 9 shortcut-map values = 45 options
  (schwa is the only PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA value not already in the
  teaching set).
- The per-chip augmentation (`BASE ∪ chips.map(c => c.p)`) still
  runs, so any draft carrying a custom phoneme the user put in
  earlier still appears in the dropdown.
- `PHONEME_CODE_TO_IPA` and `IPA_TO_PHONEME_CODE` still exist —
  they're used by filter UIs that prefer teacher-friendly plain text
  for grapheme lookups; unrelated to this surface.

TDD: failing test asserts the dropdown has ≥40 options and that
AUS-specific phonemes the 9-entry map omitted (`ɒ`, `ɜː`, `iː`, `ə`)
are all present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
```

 `./.claude/worktrees/bold-sinoussi-60d7fa/src/data/words/authoring/engine.ts`

```text
1	import { arpabetStringToIpa } from './arpabet-to-ipa';
2	
3	export interface Breakdown {
4	  word: string;
5	  ipa: string;
6	  syllables: string[];
7	  phonemes: string[];
8	  ritaKnown: boolean;
9	}
10	
11	const splitSyllablesByPhonemeBoundary = (
12	  word: string,
13	  phonemes: string[],
14	  phonemeSyllables: string[],
15	): string[] => {
16	  if (phonemeSyllables.length <= 1) return [word];
17	
18	  const lettersPerPhoneme = word.length / phonemes.length;
19	  const boundaries: number[] = [];
20	  let acc = 0;
21	  for (let i = 0; i < phonemeSyllables.length - 1; i += 1) {
22	    acc += phonemeSyllables[i]!.split('-').filter(Boolean).length;
23	    boundaries.push(Math.round(acc * lettersPerPhoneme));
24	  }
25	
26	  const out: string[] = [];
27	  let prev = 0;
28	  for (const b of boundaries) {
29	    const cut = Math.min(Math.max(b, prev + 1), word.length - 1);
30	    out.push(word.slice(prev, cut));
31	    prev = cut;
32	  }
33	  out.push(word.slice(prev));
34	  return out.filter((s) => s.length > 0);
35	};
36	
37	export const generateBreakdown = async (
38	  rawWord: string,
39	): Promise<Breakdown> => {
40	  const word = rawWord.trim().toLowerCase();
41	  const { RiTa } = await import('rita');
42	
43	  if (!RiTa.hasWord(word)) {
44	    return {
45	      word,
46	      ipa: '',
47	      syllables: [],
48	      phonemes: [],
49	      ritaKnown: false,
50	    };
51	  }
52	
53	  const phonesStr = RiTa.phones(word, { silent: true });
54	  const syllStr = RiTa.syllables(word, { silent: true });
55	  const phonemes = arpabetStringToIpa(phonesStr);
56	  const phonemeSyllables = syllStr.split('/');
57	  const syllables = splitSyllablesByPhonemeBoundary(
58	    word,
59	    phonemes,
60	    phonemeSyllables,
61	  );
62	
63	  return {
64	    word,
65	    ipa: phonemes.join(''),
66	    syllables,
67	    phonemes,
68	    ritaKnown: true,
69	  };
70	};
71
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-24 03:20:57Z)**_

You're right — Rita's LTS (letter-to-sound) engine returns guessed phones/syllables even when `hasWord` is false. The engine currently early-returns before trying. Let me plan the sequence:

**Three commits:**

1. **Engine LTS fallback**: `generateBreakdown` always tries `RiTa.phones/syllables`; `ritaKnown` stays `false` for dictionary misses but we expose the guesses.
2. **Chip alignment uses phonemes regardless of `ritaKnown`**: prothesis gets real aligned chips (low-confidence), bootstrap only for truly empty returns.
3. **Stateful syllables**: state, bootstrap, split/delete, editor popover, TDD'd.
